pin.enterDescription: Use the positions shown on your KeepKey to enter your PIN
pin.createDescription: Look at your KeepKey screen and tap the positions to set a new PIN
pin.confirmDescription: Enter the same PIN again to confirm
pin.wipeWarningDescription: Wiping your KeepKey will erase all keys and settings. You will lose access to your funds if you don't have your recovery phrase.
pin.wipeAcknowledge: I have backed up my recovery phrase and understand what I am doing

passphrase.title: Enter Passphrase
passphrase.description: Your device has passphrase protection enabled. Enter your BIP-39 passphrase to unlock. Leave empty for the default wallet.
passphrase.warning: Passphrases are case-sensitive. A different passphrase generates a completely different wallet.

pairing.wantsToConnect: wants to connect to your KeepKey
pairing.keyboardHint: Enter to approve · Esc to reject

signing.title: Signing Request
signing.keyboardHint: Enter to approve · Esc to reject

recovery.title: Recover Your Wallet
recovery.description: Look at the scrambled letters on your KeepKey and type the matching position
recovery.helpParagraph1: Your KeepKey shows a scrambled alphabet on its screen. Each letter on the device maps to a different letter on the computer keyboard.
recovery.helpParagraph2: To enter a word, find each letter of your seed word on the device screen, then press the letter shown at that position on your computer. You only need the first 3-4 characters of each word.
recovery.helpParagraph3: This way, your actual seed phrase is never typed on the computer, protecting you from keyloggers and screen capture malware.
recovery.helpParagraph4: The scramble changes for each character, so always look at the device before pressing the next key.
recovery.pinHelp1: Your KeepKey displays a scrambled number grid on its screen.
recovery.pinHelp2: Tap the positions on the computer that match the numbers you want on the device.
recovery.pinHelp3: Both PIN entries must use the same positions, even though the device scrambles the grid differently each time.
recovery.cipherHelp1: Your KeepKey shows a scrambled alphabet on its screen. Each letter on the device maps to a different letter on your keyboard.
recovery.cipherHelp2: To enter a word, find each letter of your seed word on the device screen, then press the key at that position on your computer.
recovery.cipherHelp3: The scramble changes after every character, so always check the device screen before pressing the next key.
recovery.cipherHelp4: Make sure you are entering the correct BIP39 seed words in the correct order.
recovery.recoveryInterrupted: The recovery process was interrupted. You can safely try again.

claimed.title: Device In Use
claimed.description: Your KeepKey was detected but is currently claimed by another application (e.g. KeepKey Desktop, a browser extension, or scdaemon).
claimed.step1: 1. Close other apps using your KeepKey
claimed.waiting: Waiting for device to become available...
